_comment: Hestia governance hooks for the Google Gemini CLI occupant. Gemini reads hooks from the 'hooks' object in ~/.gemini/settings.json (user), <project>/.gemini/settings.json (project), or an extension-shipped hooks/hooks.json (Extensions priority). INSTALL AT USER LEVEL: Project-source hooks are gated behind isTrustedFolder(), so a project-scoped gate does not run in an untrusted folder. Merge this 'hooks' block into settings.json for a manual install, or ship this file inside a Gemini extension. The BeforeTool gate is FAIL-CLOSED (Gemini's engine fails open on unexpected failure, and exit 1 is ALLOW+warning, so the gate carries a top-level deny-on-exception); it covers run_shell_command + file-write/replace + MCP calls + EGRESS (web_fetch/google_web_search). Only LOCAL read-class tools skip the society-safety governor. Commands bake HESTIA_WORKSPACE because the gate default (~/ai-workspace) is wrong on this host; adjust the absolute paths per install.
_hooksConfig_note: Pin "hooksConfig": {"enabled": true} in settings.json alongside this block. It defaults true, but "enabled": false disarms every hook and hooksConfig.disabled can disable this gate by command string. Operator's prerogative, but it must be visible to anything auditing the member.
